ddls:
CREATE TABLE "proteins" (
  "entry_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each protein entry */,
  "entry_name" TEXT /* Name of the protein entry */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the protein entry (e.g., reviewed, unreviewed) */,
  "protein_names" TEXT /* Names associated with the protein, including aliases */,
  "gene_names" TEXT /* Names of genes associated with the protein */,
  "organism" TEXT /* Scientific name of the organism associated with the protein */,
  "length" INTEGER /* Length of the protein sequence in amino acids */,
  "functional_category" TEXT /* Functional category of the protein */,
  "annotation_details" TEXT /* Detailed annotations for the protein */,
  "citation" TEXT /* Citation or reference to scientific publications */,
  "date_last_modified" TEXT /* Date when the protein entry was last modified */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the protein entry is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_proteins_organism FOREIGN KEY ("organism") REFERENCES organisms ("organism_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_proteins_status FOREIGN KEY ("status") REFERENCES statuses ("status_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "genes" (
  "gene_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each gene */,
  "gene_name" TEXT /* Name of the gene */,
  "entry_id" TEXT /* Reference to the associated protein entry */,
  "organism" TEXT /* Scientific name of the organism the gene belongs to */,
  "chromosome" TEXT /* Chromosome where the gene is located */,
  "start_position" INTEGER /* Start position of the gene on the chromosome */,
  "end_position" INTEGER /* End position of the gene on the chromosome */,
  "strand" TEXT /* Strand of the gene (positive or negative) */,
  "is_functional" INTEGER /* Indicates if the gene is functional */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gene_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genes_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES proteins ("entry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "organisms" (
  "organism_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each organism */,
  "organism_name" TEXT /* Scientific name of the organism */,
  "common_name" TEXT /* Common name of the organism */,
  "taxonomic_id" INTEGER /* Taxonomic ID for the organism */,
  "genus" TEXT /* Genus of the organism */,
  "species" TEXT /* Species of the organism */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("organism_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "statuses" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the status (e.g., reviewed, unreviewed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of what the status indicates */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "researchers" (
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each researcher */,
  "researcher_name" TEXT /* Full name of the researcher */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the researcher */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the researcher in the project */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the researcher belongs to */,
  "institution" TEXT /* Institution the researcher is affiliated with */,
  "date_joined" TEXT /* Date when the researcher joined the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "entry_id" TEXT /* ID of the protein entry accessed */,
  "researcher_id" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher accessing the entry */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the entry was accessed */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action performed (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the researcher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES proteins ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_researcher_id FOREIGN KEY ("researcher_id") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "version_history" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each version entry */,
  "entry_id" TEXT /* Reference to the protein entry */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the protein entry */,
  "modification_date" TEXT /* Date when the modification was made */,
  "modification_details" TEXT /* Details about the modification made */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the researcher who made the modification */,
  "reason_for_change" TEXT /* Reason for the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_entry_id FOREIGN KEY ("entry_id") REFERENCES proteins ("entry_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_version_history_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES researchers ("researcher_id")
)
